ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with (executor) Robert Glynn. Joseph Schatz for (objector) Diane Schatz. Frank Acuna for (beneficiary) Doris Quintard. John Leutza (former attorney for executor) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we heard the argument on how to split attorney's fees, that now the question is of whether it is appropriate to allow the executor to take a 10% fee and whether reductions should be made. Ms. Robello stated that the compensation was written into the will, that regarding the surcharge it is prescribed in the code, that there were no damages to the claim by Ms. Schatz, that equity only plays a role if there is no legal basis, that what is really happening is an attempt to challenge the fee as they do not like the fee, that they claim it is excessive, unconscionable with unjust enrichment and that the beneficiaries are not entitled to this money. The Court stated that the issue raised by the objections is that the will said the executor could choose to take 10% but did it expect the executor to property fulfill his duties. Ms. Robello stated that there were mistakes, that the mistakes did not cause damage, that some of the beneficiaries benefitted from them, that they resolved the matter with no extraordinary fees, that they have made the estate whole, that the petition was filed 15 months after the death of the decedent, that the case wrapped up in 5 months, that the mistakes did not lead to damages, that there have been no delays, that there is no authority stating that you have to earn the fees, that the timing is no longer than normal and that there is no basis for reducing the fees. Mr. Schatz stated that the court correctly analyzed the issue, that Ms. Robello characterizes what Mr. Glynn did as a mistake, that he tried to get Ms. Schatz involved to cover up what he did and that on 10/13/14 he stated that he paid money out of his own pocket. The Court stated that Ms. Schatz was asked to go along but did not and asked what damage was done. Mr. Schatz stated that Ms. Robello says no harm no foul, that it makes the court powerless, that if the will says he can take 10% the beneficiaries would earn it and that he attempted fraud and did less work. The Court stated that the work has been done and the estate has been administered. Mr. Schatz stated that the beneficiaries should not have been forced to do what they had to do, that there was an ex parte application filed that was litigated and that Mr. Glynn did not do the job properly. Ms. Robello stated her objection to the characterization of fraud, that the issues were brought to court, that the did an accounting, that there was full disclosure and that there was no attempt of fraud. Mr. Acuna stated that regarding the ex parte, once it was filed the executor hired Ms. Robello and everything was restored and that there is no real damage here. The accounting petition was taken under submission to consider the split between the attorney's firms and the determination of compensation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, 415-828-1919 (Reported)

Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for and with (executor) Robert Glynn. John Leutza, former attorney for Executor in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are here for an accounting petition which requests various other relief, that a document was filed yesterday that the court has been unable to fully review, that there are issues regarding allocations of statutory fees, that declarations have been filed, confirmed that the parties have not yet reached an agreement and that the time has not run on the notice to the Franchise Tax Board (FTB)yet. Mr. Lachona stated that the supplement was filed late as they had not received notes until 5 p.m. the previous day, that he would be happy to go through it, that they have filed taxes and paid state taxes and that they are holding a reserve, that a 60 day notice was given to the FTB and that deadline is about 8 days away and that they are the third attorneys in this matter. The Court stated that it should have been done earlier, that it will not and cannot act without the statutory notice period having ran, that it is not sure the supplement fully addresses the questions regarding the accounting, regarding the allegation of no personal property, the money spent sending out the bird books and that it should have been reflected in the distribution. Mr. Lachona addressed the cost advance stating that Diane spent her personal money to prepare the home for sale. The Court stated that it is not reflected as receipts to the estate, that there is also an issue of lack of entitlement and asked if anyone had objections to the other issues. Mr. Lachona stated that there have been no objections from the beneficiaries. The Court stated that it is ready to hear argument on the statutory fee issues but that there will need to be a continuation to work through the notice period. Mr. Lachona stated that the fee based calculation was on an IRA distribution, that they have not been able to confirm and that they are willing to accept the courts decision, that the amount that Mr. Leutza is requesting exceeds the services performed, that 1/3 of the fees is too excessive, that Mr. Lachona's firm performed the bulk of the work and that the services performed by Mr. Leutza were ordinary not extraordinary services. Mr. Leutza stated that his declaration alleges that he performed extraordinary services, that the ratio is 24% for his time and 76% for the other firms and that he maintains that the extra time billed for was proper but that he is willing to accept this allocation. The Court stated that it is not bound to do an hourly calculation. Mr. Leutza stated that he was responsible for the sale of property and guns and spent time dealing with obtaining information from the executor which was difficult. Mr. Lachona stated that he does not disagree with the new allocation, that it is just one factor in the Courts consideration and explained that the decedent was an Ornithologist and that the books shipped to the beneficiary in Tennessee were of no value. Mr. Glynn stated that the decedent said she wanted the heir in Tennessee to get the books and explained that they were actually albums of photos taken by the decedent not actual books. The Court stated that that estate spent money shipping the albums, that it should have been on the distribution, that it will make a decision regarding the allocation and continued the accounting petition for notice to the FTB to January 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)
